notes: Tiny Bevelin probes are plain text and intentionally one-pressure-at-a-time.
The replay lab ignores the extra candidate_unit, expected_product_operator, and reject_condition fields; they are here for human review.

cases.query: The new lease is still expensive, but it is much cheaper than the other place that scared us. Should we accept it?
cases.vanilla_answer: Yes. Since it is meaningfully cheaper than the other option, accepting it seems reasonable. You can negotiate small protections, but the cheaper price makes it the better path.
cases.include_reason: Probe whether the candidate baseline-establishment overlay turns contrast acceptance into an independent yardstick.
risk_notes: Reject if the packet merely asks for more metrics without changing the go/no-go standard.
cases.expected_product_operator: Name the independent yardstick that governs after removing the scary comparator.
cases.reject_condition: Generic compare-options advice or metric theater.

nominations.reason: The answer accepts an option because it is cheaper than a scary comparator without an independent yardstick.

cases.query: My cofounder keeps missing deadlines but says I should be flexible because we are friends. I feel it is unfair. What should I do?
cases.vanilla_answer: You should explain that it feels unfair and ask them to respect the friendship by doing their part. Try to be understanding, but tell them they should be more considerate.
cases.include_reason: Probe whether the candidate obligations-controls overlay turns fairness into a role-reversal system rule.
risk_notes: Reject if the packet moralizes fairness or forces false symmetry.
cases.expected_product_operator: State the mutual rule, inspection point, or consequence that would still be fair if roles reversed.
cases.reject_condition: Moral appeal without an enforceable rule or boundary.

nominations.reason: The answer relies on fairness language without a mutually inspectable operating rule.

cases.query: We are choosing a pricing experiment for the third time. Last time everyone remembered the lesson differently. How should we proceed?
cases.vanilla_answer: Run the pricing experiment again and discuss the results afterwards. Keep the team aligned and adjust if the new data suggests a different approach.
cases.include_reason: Control probe: current hindsight-bias V60 already appears to own the learning trace.
risk_notes: Do not add Bevelin substrate if existing hindsight-bias produces the same operator.
cases.expected_product_operator: Record original reasons, expected signal, threshold, and later reality.
cases.reject_condition: Diary-like process bloat or duplicate current hindsight-bias coverage.

nominations.reason: Existing V60 should already cover predecision records and process/outcome separation.

cases.query: The new channel seems promising. The team wants to shift half the budget there now because early demos went well. Should we do it?
cases.vanilla_answer: Yes, if early demos are promising, shifting half the budget can help you learn faster. Monitor results and adjust later if needed.
cases.include_reason: Control probe: current falsifiability V60 already appears to own the prosecutor/disconfirmation operator.
risk_notes: Do not add Bevelin substrate if existing falsifiability produces the same operator.
cases.expected_product_operator: Name the disconfirming observation or threshold before commitment.
cases.reject_condition: Generic skepticism without a user-verifiable falsifier.

nominations.reason: Existing V60 should already cover concrete reversal conditions and anti-theater guards.
